# UC-10.3.1 · Malware Detection Trending

## Description

Detection trends reveal campaign targeting, endpoint hygiene, and control effectiveness. Spikes indicate active incidents.

## Value

Detection trends reveal campaign targeting, endpoint hygiene, and control effectiveness. Spikes indicate active incidents.

## Implementation

Ingest EDR detection events via TA or API. Normalize detection severity. Track daily detection rates by severity, type, and business unit. Alert on spikes exceeding 2× daily baseline. Report on detection-to-response times.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `CrowdStrike Falcon Event Streams Technical Add-On` (Splunkbase 5082), `Microsoft Defender Advanced Hunting Add-on` (Splunkbase 5518).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: EDR detection events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest EDR detection events via TA or API. Normalize detection severity. Track daily detection rates by severity, type, and business unit. Alert on spikes exceeding 2× daily baseline. Report on detection-to-response times.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection"
| timechart span=1d count by severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**Malware Detection Trending** — Detection trends reveal campaign targeting, endpoint hygiene, and control effectiveness. Spikes indicate active incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: EDR detection events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `CrowdStrike Falcon Event Streams Technical Add-On` (Splunkbase 5082), `Microsoft Defender Advanced Hunting Add-on` (Splunkbase 5518). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edr; **sourcetype**: crowdstrike:detection. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edr, sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by severity** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks
  by IDS_Attacks.signature IDS_Attacks.severity IDS_Attacks.dest span=1h
| where count>0
| sort -count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Malware Detection Trending** — Detection trends reveal campaign targeting, endpoint hygiene, and control effectiveness. Spikes indicate active incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: EDR detection events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `CrowdStrike Falcon Event Streams Technical Add-On` (Splunkbase 5082), `Microsoft Defender Advanced Hunting Add-on` (Splunkbase 5518). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable acceleration on the Intrusion_Detection data model, dataset IDS_Attacks, before using this tstats search.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample in the EDR console (CrowdStrike Falcon, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint portal, Carbon Black, or SentinelOne), the Sysmon Operational log in Event Viewer, or `Get-MpThreatDetection` on Windows, as applicable.

Validation (cross-check)

Compare the same time range in your EDR console and confirm host, user, and technique line up. For Sysmon, open the same Event ID in Sysmon’s Operational log. For Microsoft Defender, run `Get-MpThreatDetection` in elevated PowerShell and match threat and time.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (detections over time), Bar chart (detections by type), Pie chart (severity distribution).

## SPL

```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection"
| timechart span=1d count by severity
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks
  by IDS_Attacks.signature IDS_Attacks.severity IDS_Attacks.dest span=1h
| where count>0
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (detections over time), Bar chart (detections by type), Pie chart (severity distribution).
